<!-- 
This file contains the call for papers table for the research page.
To edit call for papers information:
1. Update the 'call_for_papers' list in _callforpapers_data.py
2. Add, remove, or modify entries following the existing format
3. Each entry needs: journal, impact, due_date, topic, and website
4. The 'impact' field should contain the Journal Impact Factor (JIF) from JCR/Clarivate
   - Use empty string ("") if impact factor is unavailable
   - For multi-journal calls with separate submissions to each journal, separate values with " / " (e.g., "19.4 / 87.3")
   - For collection calls spanning multiple journals, use the primary or most representative journal's impact factor
5. The table will automatically update based on this data

This file is included in research/index.qmd and renders on the research page.
-->

## Calls for Papers

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from IPython.display import display, HTML


# PROJECTS in need of a (home) call for paper
# "https://github.com/mcphersonlab/cost-amr", 
# "https://github.com/mcphersonlab/roi-pipeline-antimicrobial"
# "https://github.com/mcphersonlab/biophysics-proteins-as-complex-machines-of-physics"
# "https://github.com/mcphersonlab/mgx-mbx-colorectal-cancer"
# "https://github.com/mcphersonlab/ai-microbiome-transmission",
# "https://github.com/mcphersonlab/structure-function-pharmacology"


# Call for papers data is imported from _callforpapers_data.py
from _callforpapers_data import call_for_papers
            
def create_callforpapers_html(papers_data):
    current_date = datetime.now()
    cutoff_date = current_date - timedelta(days=180)

    # Parse dates and sort (most recent first, then by journal name)
    # Convert "Ongoing" to a far future date for sorting purposes
    for paper in papers_data:
        if paper['due_date'] == 'Ongoing':
            paper['sort_date'] = datetime(9999, 12, 31)
            paper['display_date'] = 'Ongoing'
        else:
            paper['sort_date'] = datetime.strptime(paper['due_date'], '%Y-%m-%d')
            paper['display_date'] = paper['sort_date'].strftime('%B %d, %Y')

    # Remove entries more than 6 months past their due date
    filtered_papers = [
        p for p in papers_data
        if p['due_date'] == 'Ongoing' or p['sort_date'] >= cutoff_date
    ]

    # Sort by date (earliest first), but put "Ongoing" at the end
    sorted_papers = sorted(filtered_papers, key=lambda x: (x['sort_date'], x['journal']))
    
    html = """
    <style>
        .callforpapers-table {
            width: 100%;
            border-collapse: collapse;
            margin: 20px 0;
            font-size: 14px;
            box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);
        }
        .callforpapers-table thead tr {
            background-color: #dc3545;
            color: white;
            text-align: left;
            font-weight: bold;
        }
        .callforpapers-table th,
        .callforpapers-table td {
            padding: 12px 15px;
            border: 1px solid #ddd;
        }
        .callforpapers-table tbody tr {
            border-bottom: 1px solid #dddddd;
        }
        .callforpapers-table tbody tr:nth-of-type(even) {
            background-color: #f3f3f3;
        }
        .callforpapers-table tbody tr:hover {
            background-color: #f8d7da;
            cursor: pointer;
        }
        .callforpapers-table tbody tr.upcoming-deadline {
            background-color: #fff3cd;
            border-left: 4px solid #ffc107;
        }
        .callforpapers-table tbody tr.upcoming-deadline:hover {
            background-color: #ffe69c;
        }
        .callforpapers-table tbody tr.past-deadline {
            opacity: 0.45;
        }
        .callforpapers-table tbody tr.no-project {
            background-color: #fce0c8;
            border-left: 4px solid #cc5500;
        }
        .callforpapers-table tbody tr.no-project:hover {
            background-color: #f8c9a8;
        }
    </style>
    <table class="callforpapers-table">
        <thead>
            <tr>
                <th>Journal</th>
                <th>Impact</th>
                <th>Due Date</th>
                <th>Topic</th>
                <th>Project</th>
            </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
    """
    
    for paper in sorted_papers:
        # Determine row classes based on due date status and project presence
        classes = []
        if paper['due_date'] != 'Ongoing':
            days_until = (paper['sort_date'] - current_date).days
            if days_until < 0:
                classes.append("past-deadline")
            elif 0 <= days_until <= 60:
                classes.append("upcoming-deadline")
        
        projects = paper.get('projects', [])
        has_project = bool(projects and isinstance(projects, list) and any(p for p in projects))
        if not has_project:
            classes.append("no-project")
        
        row_class = f' class="{" ".join(classes)}"' if classes else ""
        
        website_html = f"<a href='{paper['website']}' target='_blank' style='color: #007bff;'>Link</a>"
        # Format Journal column with journal name and website link below
        journal_cell = f"<strong>{paper['journal']}</strong><br>{website_html}"
        
        # Format Impact column
        impact = paper.get('impact', '')
        impact_cell = f'<span style="font-weight: bold;">{impact}</span>' if impact else '—'
        
        # Add project links - support multiple project-specific links
        projects = paper.get('projects', [])
        if projects and isinstance(projects, list) and len(projects) > 0:
            project_links = []
            for project_url in projects:
                project_links.append(f"<a href='{project_url}' target='_blank' style='color: #007bff;'>Link</a>")
            project_cell = "<br>".join(project_links)
        else:
            project_cell = ""
        
        html += f"""
            <tr{row_class}>
                <td>{journal_cell}</td>
                <td>{impact_cell}</td>
                <td>{paper['display_date']}</td>
                <td>{paper['topic']}</td>
                <td>{project_cell}</td>
            </tr>
        """
    
    html += """
        </tbody>
    </table>
    """
    return html

display(HTML(create_callforpapers_html(call_for_papers)))
```

And other great journals across: 

**Science**: 
- [*Nature*](https://www.nature.com/)
- [*Science*](https://www.science.org/)
- [*Cell*](https://www.cell.com/)

**Medicine**: 
- [*The Lancet*](https://www.thelancet.com)
- [*New England Journal of Medicine*](https://www.nejm.org)
- [*Journal of American Medical Association (JAMA)*](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jama)
- [*Nature Medicine*](https://www.nature.com/nm/)

**Pharmacy**: 
- [*American Journal of Health-System Pharmacy (AJHP)*](https://academic.oup.com/ajhp)
- [*American Journal of Pharmaceutical Education (AJPE)*](https://www.ajpe.org/)

**Pharmacology**
- [*Nature Reviews Drug Discovery*](https://www.nature.com/nrd/)
- [*Pharmacological Reviews*](https://pharmrev.aspetjournals.org/)
- [*Trends in Pharmacological Sciences*](https://www.cell.com/trends/pharmacological-sciences/home)

**Chemistry**: 
- [*JACS*](https://pubs.acs.org/journal/jacsat)
- [*JCTC*](https://pubs.acs.org/journal/jctcce) 
- [*JCIM*](https://pubs.acs.org/journal/jcisd8)
- [*Chemical Reviews*](https://pubs.acs.org/journal/chreay)

**Infectious Diseases**: 
- [*Clinical Infectious Diseases (CID)*](https://academic.oup.com/cid)
- [*Nature Microbiology*](https://www.nature.com/nmicrobiol/)
- [*Nature Reviews Microbiology*](https://www.nature.com/nrmicro/)
- [*The Lancet Infectious Diseases*](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/laninf/home)
- [*The Lancet Microbe*](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lanmic/home)
- [*The Lancet HIV*](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lanhiv/home)
- [*Cell Host and Microbe*](https://www.cell.com/cell-host-microbe/home)
- [*Antimicrobial Agents and Chemotherapy (AAC)*](https://journals.asm.org/journal/aac)
- [*Clinical Microbiology Reviews (CMR)*](https://journals.asm.org/journal/cmr)
- [*Journal of Virology*](https://journals.asm.org/journal/jvi)
- [*Clinical Microbiology and Infection (CMI)*](https://www.clinicalmicrobiologyandinfection.org/)
- [*Journal of Antimicrobial Chemotherapy (JAC)*](https://academic.oup.com/jac)
- [*Anaerobe*](https://www.sciencedirect.com/journal/anaerobe)
